question_text:
Read N Characters Given Read4 II - Call Multiple Times

Given a file and assume that you can only read the file using a given method read4, implement a method read to read n characters. Your method read may be called multiple times.

read4 reads 4 consecutive characters from the file, copies them into buffer buf4, and returns the number of characters actually read.

Implement method read: reads n characters from the file and copies to buf — returns the number of characters actually read.

Constraints:

  • 1 <= file.length <= 500
  • 1 <= queries.length <= 10
  • 1 <= queries[i] <= 500
input_format: Commands ["Solution","read","read",...], args [[],[n],[n],...] for multiple reads from a file.
output_format: List of results: null for constructor, integer (chars actually read) for each read call.
explanation:
Internal Buffer Queue

Maintain a persistent queue/buffer across calls. Each call to read: drain leftover chars first, then call read4 only when more are needed, buffer any excess. Track file position with a class-level buffer and pointer. This handles the case where read4 pulls ahead of what was requested.

Time: O(N) per call. Space: O(1) internal buffer.
